source.notes: Optimus _ Dynamic Connections - 2026_05_14 15_29 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Discovery/demo call where Max Riemer (Optimus) pitched a freight-prospecting platform to A.J. Todd of Dynamic Connections, a specialized 3PL (trade shows, refrigerated, cannabis, pharma, Canada cross-border white-glove). Max ran a prepared, competent product walkthrough positioned as a ZoomInfo replacement at ~$1,500/mo, but the platform's data skews toward dry van/reef/flatbed and admittedly thins out on the specialized verticals that define this prospect. The call ended with an unscheduled, buyer-owned next step: A.J. to think through unique use cases and follow up, with Max to chase if he doesn't hear back early next week.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max came prepared with pre-call research and stayed responsive throughout, adapting to A.J.'s answers (e.g., pivoting to the under-50k shipper filter when A.J. flagged an SMB focus) rather than reciting a script; his acoustics back this up with strong pitch variation (9.57 st) and steadier-than-baseline delivery (loudness CV z -1.1).

evidence.quote: I was just on your LinkedIn. You were Coyote and
I know it's uh DOT week, so a lot of people I'm speaking with are going nuts right now.

value_prop_clarity.explanation: A neutral listener could summarize it: replace ZoomInfo with contacts plus real invoice/shipment data that shows which shippers run which lanes, and he framed a concrete ROI against the ~$1,500/mo price.

evidence.quote: we do confidently tell people now that with our contacts, you could replace Zoom Info
if you have 15 guys that are selling 80% of the time and they able to close one customer a month from Optimus, then you know, you probably more than pay for it in a month or so

relevance.explanation: A.J. described a specialized, heavily Canada-cross-border network (trade shows, cannabis, pharma, white-glove), yet Max ran a generic US dry-van demo and conceded the data thins out precisely on those specialized verticals, leaving the buyer to figure out fit rather than tailoring the pitch to their lanes.

evidence.quote: Equipment types, uh, drive van, reef, or flatbed. Uh, there's just not a whole lot of data that we have on others.
a lot of those specialized carriers probably aren't using factoring
if we wanted to search Chattanooga to Chicago, looking for a specific lane, maybe it's a backhole opportunity

discovery_progression.explanation: Early discovery was genuinely good (team size, selling ratio, current tools, ICP), but Max never converted it into a concrete dated next step, ending with a vague buyer-owned follow-up and offering to chase only if he hears nothing.

evidence.quote: what's sort of like what's the process been as far as prospecting, finding emails, phone numbers, pitches?
you shoot me over those and, um, yeah, let's just make sure it's going to be something that your guys are going to be able to use

Max Riemer.coaching_action: For the next conversation, lead by pulling their actual business into the tool live — search their real lanes (Canada cross-border, pharma, refrigerated) on-screen to prove or disprove data coverage — and close by booking a specific follow-up date instead of leaving it open.

signals.signal: Named a concrete capability he found valuable (lane search / shipper density)
signals.quote: if you're able to search lanes and uh identify, you know, which clients are shipping in what lanes, create density, that's super helpful

signals.signal: Probing question depth on data provenance
signals.quote: how do you populate like the actual like Kroger information in there? Is it all all just from

signals.signal: Soft, deflected commitment plus a contract-timing hedge, and put the onus back on the seller to follow up (consistent with arousal fading across the call)
signals.quote: if you don't hear from me early next week, just shoot me a note
